naive_cost: A generic pass over all 15 open nodes would spend up to 900 solver-seconds and mix stale-orbit cleanup, an unmeasured case, and twelve established timeouts before review.
opportunities_considered: Remeasure the two stale orbit-discovery leaves under the active blocker.
Measure the sole never-tested active-frontier node.
Increase timeouts uniformly on the twelve-node hard tail.
Resume broad sequential harvesting or kmtotalizer.
chosen_reductions: Run exactly t-16, t-17, and s-r0-1 once with sequential encoding at 60 seconds each. The selected set is disjoint from the complete 32-certificate index and bound to the audited seven-orbit blocker and frontier hashes.
expected_throughput_gain: Avoids all twelve known timeout cases until their shared structure is profiled; resolves the two stale catalogue interactions and sole unmeasured case in at most 180 solver-seconds.
soundness_basis: The complete durable certificate index, 47-node frontier identity, 2,576-clause active blocker, exact three-node selection, and immutable manifest hashes pass independent preflight. Every UNSAT requires CNF audit and DRAT replay; every SAT requires direct validation and canonicalization.
remains_uncompressed: The twelve established timeout cases remain a structural hard-tail problem. The seven-orbit catalogue remains non-exhaustive even if all three selected leaves close.
